hypothesis_en: Mr President, it is clear that the conclusions of the Spring Summit have been marked by the terrorist attack in Madrid, an attack which has had not only political consequences but also obvious economic consequences, given the doses of uncertainty, instability and insecurity that every terrorist phenomenon entails, as the citizens of Madrid who have suffered this terrible attack and as the Spanish citizens of the Basque Country also know very well. Where, more than 20 years ago, there was a nationalist government that has not been able to guarantee the security or the most basic rights of citizens during this whole period and where, by the way, the President of this government was the first political responsible in Spain who attributed the authorship of this terrorist attack to the terrorist gang. This new climate has been created precisely as a consequence of the attitude taken by the Member States in the light of the new circumstances, including the most recalcitrant ones, and I want to insist that Spain has always been willing to negotiate to make its conditions more flexible. It is important to say, Mr President, that not only alongside the terrorist phenomenon, but also with the crisis of the Stability Pact, the appreciation of the euro and the challenges and opportunities presented by enlargement, the climate that the European Union is experiencing is not the most optimistic at the moment. Mr President, regardless of the structural reforms required by the Lisbon Strategy, the most important qualitative leap Europe has to make is the relaunch of the European project, not as a simple monetary union, not as a single market. It is not the real political project, and that is why, Mr President, we have the Presidency of the Council and, in particular, the Presidency of the European Commission.
reference_en: thank you very much the conclusions of the Spring Summit (1.55) coincided with the a= attacks in Madrid that had appalling human effects but also economic effects bear in mind the lack of security that's been brought about by any terrorist attack terrorist attack the citizens of Madrid had to suffer the effects of this terrorist outrage and then there were the citizens of the Basque country where for more than twenty years there's been a nationalist regional government that's not succeeded in guaranteeing security or even the most basic rights of the citizens over those twenty years and the president of that regional government was the in fact the earliest political leader in Spain to attribute the authorship of the Madrid attack to ETA I (1.25) have to say that I don't agree with the comments that have been made this morning in the hemicycle with regard to the change in government in Spain being linked with the change in climate I (1.13) think in fact the Member States including the most recalcitrant ones have adapted to the new circumstances I have to add that Spain has always been flexible about negotiating Europe wants and needs a new Constitution but it needs to be a Constitution in which all citizens can see themselves represented and reflected so it's important to talk not just about the terrorist phenomenon but also given the crisis involving the Stability Pact and the euro and so on it should be borne in mind that the atmosphere abroad in Europe is not an ideal atmosphere right now the press says that we've lost one point com= competitiveness vis vis the United States and (1.65) the prosperity level of the average European citizen is thirty per cent below that of the average American citizen right now (1.29) so what's to be done to get back to sustainable growth well (1.08) President now I'm concluding i= irrespective of the structural reforms that are required by the Lisbon agenda the most important point the qualitative leap forward we need is to launch our European project once again not just as a monetary project not just as a single market but as a genuinely political project and for that President we are relying upon the Presidency of the Council and particularly the Presidency of the European Commission thank you
source_es: muchas gracias señor Presidente es evidente que las conclusiones de la Cumbre de Primavera han venido marcadas por el atentado terrorista de Madrid atentado que ha tenido no sólo unas consecuencias políticas sino también unas consecuencias económicas evidentes habida cuenta de las dosis de incertidumbre de inestabilidad y de inseguridad que todo fenómeno terrorista comporta como saben muy bien los ciudadanos madrileños que han padecido este terrible atentado y como saben también muy bien los ciudadanos españoles del País Vasco donde hace más de veinte años hay un Gobierno nacionalista que no ha sido capaz de garantizar durante todo este periodo ni la seguridad ni los más elementales derechos de los ciudadanos y donde por cierto el Presidente de este Gobierno ha sido el= el primer responsable político en España que ha atribuido la la autoría de este atentado a la banda terrorista ETA yo quisiera señor Presidente decir en lo que se refiere al tema constitucional que no comparto las observaciones que se han hecho esta mañana en el hemiciclo en relación con que el cambio de Gobierno de España está en el origen del nuevo clima que se ha creado este nuevo clima se ha creado precisamente como consecuencia de la actitud que han asumido los Estados miembros a la luz de las nuevas circunstancias incluidos los más recalcitrantes y yo quiero insistir en que España siempre ha estado dispuesta a una negociación en flexibilizar sus condiciones Europa quiere y Europa necesita una Constitución pero tiene que ser una Constitución donde todos los ciudadanos europeos se puedan ver reconocidos es importante decir señor Presidente que no sólo al lado del fenómeno terrorista sino también con la crisis del Pacto de Estabilidad la apreciación del euro y los desafíos retos y oportunidades que nos presenta la ampliación que el clima que está viviendo la Unión Europea no es el clima más optimista en estos momentos la prensa destacaba que Europa ha perdido un punto de competitividad en los últimos diez años con respecto a los Estados Unidos y que hoy la prosperidad del ciudadano medio de la Unión Europea está en el treinta por ciento de la prosperidad o está tre= un treinta por ciento por debajo de la prosperidad del ciudadano medio americano cuáles son los elementos que tenemos que tener para recuperar la senda del crecimiento sostenido y la recuperación del empleo señor Presidente con independencia y acabo ya de las reformas estructurales que exige la estrategia de Lisboa lo más importante el salto cualitativo que tiene que dar Europa es el relanzamiento del proyecto europeo no como una simple unión monetaria no como s= un simple mercado único sino el verdadero proyecto político y para eso señor Presidente contamos con la Presidencia del Consejo y muy particularmente con la Presidencia de la Comisión Europea muchas gracias
